title: Samantha and the Rainbow Unicorn
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

lines: Over the rainbow,
under the stars…
Duga will find you
wherever you are.

pages.title: Samantha and the Rainbow Unicorn
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy

lines: The stars blinked softly above Samantha's bed,
As moonlight played silver round Dady Fredy's head.
"Come, anak," he whispered, "come look at the sky—
Something is coming. I hear hooves up high."
A shimmer of colors came spiraling down,
A sparkle of hooves and a mane like a crown.
Out of the clouds stepped a unicorn white,
With a horn made of moonstone that glistened with light.
"Her name," Dady whispered, "is Duga — that means
'Rainbow' in Serbian, the language of dreams."
She bowed her bright head and said, gentle and true:
"Come, mala zvezdice — I've come here for you."
Samantha climbed up and held tight to her mane,
And Dady Fredy leapt on — they were soaring again!
They rose through the clouds like a warm evening breeze,
Past sleeping volcanoes and cinnamon trees.
They galloped past Belgrade where two rivers meet,
Where Sava joins Danube beneath the moon's sheet.
Old Kalemegdan glowed golden below,
And Samantha waved down at the city's soft glow.
A bear baked pogača beneath a plum tree,
While hedgehogs served honey and wild cherry tea.
A cricket played gusle — one string, one small bow—
And everything hummed in a soft, sleepy flow.
"Look there!" Samantha laughed — butterflies all around,
With wings made of silk that made barely a sound.
Below them the oceans glowed sapphire and blue,
And islands of coral came drifting in view.
Duga ran faster through rivers of light,
Through orchards of plum trees and meadows of white.
Past mountains where eagles told stories in song,
And valleys where fireflies flickered along.
But even bright unicorns slow down their stride,
When dream journeys end and it's time for the ride
Back home through the silver and soft evening air,
With moonlight like diamonds caught bright in their hair.
She carried them gently back down through the sky,
Where stars blinked like candles and clouds drifted by.
Duga knelt softly on grass wet with dew,
And whispered, "Laku noć, zvezdice — I'll dream of you, too."
And Dady Fredy held Samantha so tight,
And whispered the words he says every night:
"Wherever you wander, anak… wherever you roam—
My Samantha, my zvezdice… you're always home."
So close your bright eyes now, my Samantha, my dear,
The unicorn's rainbow is resting right here.
The stars are all sleeping, the night soft and deep—
And Dady Fredy loves you.
Now drift into sleep.
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
